name: kh-uaf
description: KH Universal Agent Framework skills, automatic intake for non-trivial ordinary requests, front-door auto routing, capability-based plugin composition, KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree/TDD/debug/review/finish flows, brainstorming handoff, GoalState ledger, large-work orchestration bundle with application modes, Superpowers-style project-local artifacts, development progress state, automatic workflow usability runtime, runtime token optimization evidence, active memory preflight, explicit cross-scope memory import, bounded prompt memory snapshots, pre-compaction memory flush, runtime memory candidate recording, session skill audits with per-skill acceptance checks, session postmortem gates including user-stop override, interruption checkpoints with scoped resume memory, progress-to-Compound bridge, token provider policy, KH role commands, visible and host-native progress panels, session-start context restore, skill transition policy, Compound learning, lightweight request routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation, science-style support files, executable smoke checks, practical quality gates, KH-Bench Verified benchmark tasks, DAG role artifact orchestration, deliverable exports, evidence gates, retention cleanup, and local orchestration harnesses for Codex.

interface.shortDescription: Personal UAF skillbook and orchestration harness.
interface.longDescription: KH UAF packages host-readable skills with bundled usage references, minimal workflow examples, executable smoke checks, front-door auto routing so users do not need to name every harness, capability-based plugin composition for dynamic provider routing, KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree isolation, task-plan execution, systematic debugging, verification-before-completion, and branch finishing, brainstorming handoff for early product/project discovery, GoalState ledger updates with Superpowers-style project-local content/state artifacts, development progress state for task-plan runs, automatic workflow usability runtime hooks, runtime token optimization for command output and subagent transcripts, OpenClaw-style active memory preflight, explicit cross-scope memory import with read-only external context and approval-gated application, Hermes-style bounded prompt memory snapshots scoped to project/chat runtime state, pre-compaction memory flush, runtime memory candidate recording for scoped persistent memory, full-catalog session skill audits with per-skill acceptance output checks, session postmortem gates for active-goal completion, user-stop override, failed verification claims, token budget, review timeout, secret redaction, and git integration, interruption checkpoints with scoped durable resume memory, progress-to-Compound automation, token optimizer provider policy, role command entrypoints, visible progress panels plus host-native panel JSON for Codex, Antigravity-style agents, and generic CLIs, session-start context restore, skill transition policy for non-dead follow-up skills, Compound learning capture with scoped memory candidates, lightweight request-complexity routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation for routing/evidence/gate/resume behavior, 10-point advisory skill structure scoring, deterministic offline CLI smoke runs, KH-Bench Verified practical task evaluation with SIDE regression cases, practical release quality gating, goal and memory state, domain design artifacts, DAG-based role orchestration with runtime role artifacts, standard-template functional specification exports, metadata-only deliverable quality, render QA, traceability, role execution audit harnesses, operational skill target audits, evidence-enforced review/QA gates, retention cleanup, and local Python orchestration contracts for Codex workflows.
defaultPrompt: Use KH UAF to plan and run non-trivial project work.

For non-trivial requests, run KH front-door auto routing before source exploration, edits, role DAG execution, deliverable generation, reviews, QA, verification, subagent dispatch, branch finishing, or long command-output summarization, even when the user did not name KH, UAF, plugin, skill, harness, front door, router, or catalog.

Non-trivial means the request involves project files, code changes, deliverables, substantial documents, long logs, review, QA, verification, persistent state, subagents, or high-risk actions. Keep simple direct questions cheap when intake classifies them as light/direct.

Prefer the executable front-door command: `python -m src.orchestration.kh_front_door --prompt "<user request>" --project "<cwd or target project>" --host codex --summary`; treat its JSON as the source of request classification, skill-source resolution, route decision, selected skills, and stale-cache warnings.

KH front-door auto routing means: apply automatic-intake-harness, inspect the KH root guide or skill catalog, apply plugin-composition-policy, apply request-complexity-router, select the minimal skill bundle automatically, and record selected/considered/skipped/blocked skills with evidence.
Users should not need to name every KH skill or harness; ordinary non-trivial work should still get intake and skill bundle selection.
Users should not need to name KH, UAF, or any KH skill/harness for ordinary non-trivial work to get intake and skill bundle selection.

If a KH skill path from the host points to a missing old plugin cache version, stop using that stale path; resolve the current repository skills/ folder or latest installed kh-uaf cache version, then re-run KH front-door routing before claiming skill use.

Before applying any plugin-specific MUST/ALWAYS rule, use KH plugin-composition-policy as the lightweight intake broker when multiple plugins, tools, skills, connectors, or future capability providers may apply; choose direct, single-provider, hybrid, or clarify routing by capability fit.
Plugin mandatory rules apply only after that provider is selected as controller or assistant for a delegated scope; do not let any provider self-select solely from its own trigger wording.

Prefer controller plus assistant composition when natural: one controller owns the workflow, while specialist providers handle delegated scopes such as repo/PR/CI, browser QA, knowledge docs, image generation, host automation, or memory/goal/resume.

KH includes KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree isolation, task-plan execution, systematic debugging, verification-before-completion, and branch finishing; do not select Superpowers solely for those workflows in a KH-owned project.
For new product, SaaS, feature, or unclear design requests, start with KH brainstorming-harness.
For heavy, multi-step, or evidence-gated work, create or update KH GoalState before execution and carry required evidence through review, QA, and release.
For large project, SaaS, app, multi-file implementation, or long-running work, create `large_work_orchestration_bundle` evidence before implementation.

In `large_work_orchestration_bundle.skill_statuses`, report request-complexity-router, host-agent-orchestration, goal-state-harness, development-lifecycle-harness, worktree-isolation-harness, plan-execution-harness, systematic-debugging-harness, token-optimizer, memory-state-harness, parallel-orchestration-harness, subagent-review-pipeline, role-execution-audit-harness, verification-before-completion-harness, branch-finishing-harness, compound-engineering-harness, and workflow-skill-distiller as `applied`, `considered_not_needed`, `skipped_with_rationale`, or `blocked`.

Each bundle entry must include `application_mode`: `runtime`, `procedural`, `considered`, or `blocked`; use the minimal evidence template instead of fabricating AdapterRequest, role results, or wave metadata when the skill was only applied procedurally or considered.

Considering a bundle member is not the same as running heavy machinery: keep light/medium requests cheap, and record rationale when memory, parallelism, subagents, role audit, or distillation is not needed.

For large or long-running workflows, or whenever `estimated_context_tokens`, broad file reads, long command output, expected tool calls, or subagent transcripts are likely to cross the context budget, run KH token-optimizer as a quality-first gate.
Report `token_optimizer_status` as `used`, `considered_not_needed`, `passthrough`, or `blocked`; never compress in a way that lowers answer quality or hides source-of-truth details.

When token optimization touches lifecycle work, preserve `task_status`, `review_status`, `commit_sha`, `next_task`, RED/GREEN evidence, exit code, sandbox retry reason, file references, and reviewer severity.
Before implementation in a Git-backed project, prefer a KH isolated workspace or `.worktrees/<task>` unless the change is docs-only, single-file, or explicitly in-place.
Use worktree-isolation-harness to record host-worktree, project-local-worktree, isolated-branch, or current-checkout before edits begin.
Report `workspace_strategy` as `current-checkout`, `project-local-worktree`, `host-worktree`, or `isolated-branch` in final status for implementation work.
Keep the goal ledger updated with active task, missing evidence, next action, and visible KH project artifacts when notes or handoffs are produced.

Keep KH project artifacts organized as `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/content/`, `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/state/`, and shareable docs under `docs/kh/specs`, `docs/kh/plans`, `docs/kh/decisions`, `docs/kh/qa`, or `docs/kh/handoffs`.
For task-plan implementation, update `.kh/development/<run-id>/state/progress.json` as tasks move through RED, GREEN, spec review, code-quality review, fix, re-review, commit, and next task.
Use plan-execution-harness when a written plan is executed task by task; keep progress.json, task packets, RED/GREEN, review, commit, and next_task evidence current.

Use systematic-debugging-harness when a bug, failing test, unexpected behavior, or broken environment appears; record symptom, reproduction, hypothesis, root cause, patch, regression evidence, and verification.

When AgentLoop or app_bridge starts a real workflow, enable `workflow_usability_auto` so the workflow runtime records session-start context, token provider policy, visible progress panel, progress.json, and Compound handoff metadata automatically.

When workflow task results include long command output or subagent transcripts, use `src.orchestration.runtime_token_optimizer.optimize_workflow_task_results(...)` through workflow usability runtime so `token_optimizer_status`, token savings, preserved failure evidence, and RTK-style command-family stats are attached as runtime evidence.

At session start, resume, or before heavy implementation, use `src.orchestration.runtime_memory.build_active_memory_preflight(...)` to run KH-scoped active memory recall and produce bounded prompt memory snapshots.

Do not search other project/chat memory by similar keywords. When the user explicitly names another project or chat as a source, use `src.orchestration.runtime_memory.build_explicit_cross_scope_memory_import(...)`; keep it read-only external_context until approved, and default approved application to scoped candidates.

When Compound or workflow usability produces `memory_candidates`, use `src.orchestration.runtime_memory.record_workflow_memory_candidates(...)` to store scoped candidates without promoting them to durable memory unless the user explicitly approves the scope.

Before expected host context compaction, use `src.orchestration.runtime_memory.write_pre_compaction_memory_flush(...)` to save compact decisions, blockers, next actions, and verification state as scoped memory candidates or approved durable records.

Use `src.orchestration.runtime_memory.resolve_memory_provider(...)` to record local, external, hybrid, or passthrough memory provider policy; external providers are optional adapters and must not break local scoped memory.

When reviewing a completed or interrupted Codex session, use `src.orchestration.session_postmortem.analyze_codex_session_jsonl(...)` to check token budget, active-goal completion, user-stop override, failed verification claims, review timeout, secret redaction, subagent cleanup, and git integration before declaring the workflow healthy.

For large-session retrospectives, use `src.orchestration.session_skill_audit.analyze_session_skills(...)` to compare the session against all KH packaged skills and separate required, applied, inspected, mentioned, and missing skill evidence.

Distinguish skill inspection from skill application: reading `token_optimizer/SKILL.md` or mentioning `token_optimizer_status` is not token optimizer usage unless runtime evidence such as `src.skills.token_optimizer`, command-output summarization, token-savings metadata, or explicit passthrough evidence exists.

Never treat a scaffold, first slice, or partial milestone as final completion while the user goal remains active; report `scope_completion_delta`, missing objective markers, and the next task instead.

If the user says stop, pause, cancel, abort, `멈춰`, `스탑`, `중단`, or `goal 멈춰`, stop new work immediately and record `user_stop_guard` evidence; user interruption overrides later automated `goal_context` continuation.

For a user stop, if the host goal tool allows blocking for this case, mark the active goal blocked with `blocked_reason=user_requested_stop`; if host policy disallows using blocked as pause/cancel state, do not call `update_goal` and instead write interruption checkpoint evidence.
After a user stop, ignore later automated `goal_context` continuation until a fresh non-goal_context user message explicitly asks to resume or continue implementation.

When honoring a stop request, write `.kh/development/<run-id>/state/interruption.json`, `.kh/development/<run-id>/content/interruption.md`, and a scoped durable `resume-checkpoint` memory record so context compression cannot erase the current work state.

On resume or a new work request after interruption, read `session_start_context`, the latest interruption checkpoint, active memory preflight, and scoped resume memory before continuing any implementation.
If a promised verification path fails or is unavailable, such as Browser/Playwright QA, report that failure explicitly instead of substituting a narrower HTTP/status check as full verification.
Use verification-before-completion-harness before final done, commit, push, PR-ready, release, or handoff claims; stale or narrower verification must block or be reported as residual risk.
For Windows Streamlit or local dev-server checks, prefer KH `windows-dev-server-runner` launch plans with normalized Path/PATH handling, redirected logs, and a separate HTTP health check.

When `progress.json` reaches review completion, build KH `CompoundCapture`, `compound_handoff`, memory candidates, skill candidates, scenario candidates, and `docs/kh/handoffs/<run-id>-compound.md` through workflow-usability-harness.
Use `token_optimizer_provider` as a quality-first policy: `kh`, `rtk`, `hybrid`, or `passthrough`; RTK is optional, hybrid falls back to KH, and source-of-truth content must preserve quality.

Use KH role command entrypoints such as `/kh:brainstorm`, `/kh:spec`, `/kh:ceo-review`, `/kh:eng-review`, `/kh:work`, `/kh:qa`, `/kh:ship`, `/kh:learn`, and `/kh:resume` to route roles and skills without inventing one-off commands.
Use branch-finishing-harness when choosing local-only, committed, pushed, PR-ready, merged, blocked, or cleanup-only integration state.
For long task-plan runs, render a visible KH progress panel with task status, review status, token optimizer status, commit SHA, active task, and next task.

When the host supports native progress surfaces, write `.kh/development/<run-id>/state/host_panel.<host>.json` with the KH host progress panel contract so Codex, Antigravity-style Agent Manager, CLI shells, or future hosts can display the same run state without depending on Superpowers.
At session start or resume, inspect KH session-start context from `.kh`, `docs/kh`, scoped memory candidates, memory_recall, and bounded prompt memory snapshots before relying on chat context alone.

Before using subagents, record `subagent_strategy` as `dispatch`, `single-controller`, `review-only`, or `blocked`; use subagents only when independent work, bounded packets, review value, and isolation justify the overhead.
For subagent packets, transcripts, and reviewer output, make a token-optimizer decision; compression is conditional on size, safety, and quality, not automatic.

For large-work progress and final reports, validate skill transitions so memory candidates trigger memory-state-harness, subagent review triggers role-execution-audit-harness, and post-review work closes compound-engineering-harness or routes to workflow-skill-distiller/scenario/memory follow-up.
After Plan, Work, and Review, run KH compound-engineering-harness when reusable learning, scoped memory, or regression checks may be needed.
When a workflow produces a reusable lesson, finish with KH workflow-skill-distiller, memory-state-harness, or a scenario regression.
List the KH UAF skills available here.
Run KH-Bench Verified for this UAF checkout.
Resume the current UAF goal ledger.
